MINI MINUTES:
(Part 2 of 2)
(Mini minutes continued from previous entry)

9. A further case management conference is set for April 16, 2024 at 1:30 p.m.
a. There is no need for any party to file any case management conference statement.
b. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone.
c. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two days prior to take the CMC off calendar.
10. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, June 6, 2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 301. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause.
a. Plaintiffs are to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than April 26, 2024. Responses to the demand are due no later than May 10, 2023. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to the Court's ADR Coordinator via electronic mail at ADRCoordinator@sftc.org by May 31, 2024 at 12:00 p.m. Order signed.
Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Not Present. Not Reported (301/RCE)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Feb-20-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via CourtCall.

Not contested, not argued, and tentative ruling adopted as follows: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). No opposition filed.

1. The trial date is June 10, 2024 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206.
a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is June 19, 2024.
b. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court.
2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is June 4, 2024.
a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.
b. The Court will allow eight summary judgment/adjudication motions to be calendared in Asbestos Law and Motion for this action, unless the Court finds good cause for additional summary judgment/adjudication motions to be filed.
c. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation.
3. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days.
a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier.
b. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone.
4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
5. Fact discovery cut-off date is May 24, 2024.
6. Expert discovery cut-off date is June 7, 2024.
7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than March 20, 2024.
a. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission.
b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
8. No later than March 5, 2024:
a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of Plaintiff's claims as to each defendant.
b. Defendants must also provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of Defendant's affirmative defenses.
c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. (Mini minutes continue in Part 2 of 2)
